ddls:
CREATE TABLE "rentals" (
  "rental_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rental transaction */,
  "instant" INTEGER /* Sequential number for each rental transaction */,
  "dteday" TEXT /* Date of the rental transaction */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season of the year (1-4) */,
  "yr" INTEGER /* Year of the rental transaction */,
  "mnth" INTEGER /* Month of the rental transaction */,
  "hr" INTEGER /* Hour of the rental transaction */,
  "holiday" INTEGER /* Whether the day is a holiday (0/1) */,
  "weekday" INTEGER /* Day of the week (0-6) */,
  "workingday" INTEGER /* Whether the day is a working day (0/1) */,
  "weathersit" INTEGER /* Weather situation (1-4) */,
  "temp" REAL /* Temperature */,
  "atemp" REAL /* Feels-like temperature */,
  "hum" REAL /* Humidity */,
  "windspeed" REAL /* Wind speed */,
  "casual" INTEGER /* Number of casual users */,
  "registered" INTEGER /* Number of registered users */,
  "cnt" INTEGER /* Total number of rentals */,
  "start_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station where the bike was picked up */,
  "end_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station where the bike was returned */,
  "ride_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the rental in minutes */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who rented the bike */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rental_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rentals_start_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("start_station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rentals_end_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("end_station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rentals_holiday FOREIGN KEY ("holiday") REFERENCES holidays ("holiday_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rentals_weathersit FOREIGN KEY ("weathersit") REFERENCES weather_situation ("weather_situation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rentals_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rentals_season FOREIGN KEY ("season") REFERENCES seasons ("season_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rentals_weekday FOREIGN KEY ("weekday") REFERENCES weekday ("weekday_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rentals_workingday FOREIGN KEY ("workingday") REFERENCES workingday ("workingday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, station manager) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the bike-sharing service */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the station */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Total number of bike docks at the station */,
  "current_bikes_count" INTEGER /* Number of bikes currently available at the station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the station's location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the station's location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bikes" (
  "bike_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bike */,
  "bike_type" TEXT /* Type of bike */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station where the bike is located */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the bike (e.g., available, in use, maintenance) */,
  "last_service_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bike_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bikes_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rental_history" (
  "rental_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rental transaction */,
  "bike_id" INTEGER /* ID of the bike rented */,
  "rental_date" TEXT /* Date the bike was rented */,
  "return_date" TEXT /* Date the bike was returned */,
  "start_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station where the bike was picked up */,
  "end_station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station where the bike was returned */,
  "ride_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the rental in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rental_id", "bike_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the weather record */,
  "temp" REAL /* Temperature */,
  "atemp" REAL /* Feels-like temperature */,
  "hum" REAL /* Humidity */,
  "windspeed" REAL /* Wind speed */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Amount of precipitation */,
  "cloud_cover" REAL /* Percentage of cloud cover */,
  "uv_index" REAL /* UV index */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "seasons" (
  "season_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each season */,
  "season_name" TEXT /* Name of the season */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("season_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "holidays" (
  "holiday_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each holiday */,
  "holiday_date" TEXT /* Date of the holiday */,
  "holiday_name" TEXT /* Name of the holiday */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("holiday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_situation" (
  "weather_situation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather situation */,
  "weather_situation_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather situation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_situation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weekday" (
  "weekday_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weekday */,
  "weekday_name" TEXT /* Name of the weekday */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weekday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workingday" (
  "workingday_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each working day type */,
  "workingday_name" TEXT /* Name of the working day type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workingday_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the prediction */,
  "predicted_rentals" INTEGER /* Predicted number of rentals */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station for which the prediction is made */,
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather record used for prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_weather_id FOREIGN KEY ("weather_id") REFERENCES weather ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id")
)
